folio.label: 120v
folio.l: In at the halle dore , al sodeynly
There cam a knygút , vp on a steede of Bras
And in his hand , a brode Myrour of glas
Vp on his thombe , he hadde of gold a ryngö
And by his syde , a naked swerd hangyngö
And vp he ridetú , vn to the heigú bord
In al the halle , ne was there spoke a word
For mäueille of this knygút , hi~ to byholde
Ful bisily they wayten , ©onge and olde
¢ This straunge knygút , that cam thus sodeynly
Al armed saue his hed , ful richely
Saluetú kyngö and Quene , and lordes alle
By ordre , as they setyn , in the halle
Witú so heigú reuäence , and obeisaunce
As wel in speche , as in hishis countenaunce
That Gawayn , witú his olde curteisye
Thougú he were come a geyn , out of fairye
Ne coude hi~ nougút amende , witú a word
And after this , bifore the heigú bord
He witú a manly voice , seide his message
After the foåme vsed , in his langage
Witú outen vice of silable , or lettre
And for his tale , shulde seme the bettre
Accordant to his wordes , was his chiere
As techetú art of speche , hem that it lere
Al be it , that I can nougút , sowne his stile
Ne can nougút clymben , ouä so heigú a stile
ñet sey I this , that as to comune entent
Thus meche amountetú , al æ euäe he ment
If it so be , that I haue it in mynde
¢ He seide the kyngö of Arabe , and of Ynde
My lige lord , on this solempne day
Saluetú ©ow , as he best can and may
And sendetú ©ow , in honour of ©oure feste
By me , that am al redy , at ©oure heste
This steede of Bras , that esily and wel
Can in the space , of oo day naturel
That is to seyn , in foure and twenty houres
Where so ©ow list , in drougút or ellis shoures
Beren ©oure body , in to euäy place
To whicú ©oure herte wylnetú , for to pace
Witú outen wem of ©ow , thurgú foule and fair
Or if ©ow list , to fleighe as heigú in the eyr
